metadata.analyst_note: Prompt instructed CarelonRx ~60% of Carelon OI (~$2.0B) and Carelon Services ~$1.4B; actual GAAP/adj figures from earnings release are CarelonRx $2.418B (71.6% of Carelon) and Carelon Services $0.982B (29.4% of Carelon adj total $3.4B). Model uses actual reported figures. Carelon Services 3.4% margin check: 3.4% × $28.316B revenue = $963M GAAP (adj ~$982M); verified.

health_benefits.rationale: Distressed MCO multiple reflecting elevated benefit expense ratio (90.0% FY2025, +150bp YoY), Medicaid membership attrition, CMS reimbursement risk, and compressed Health Benefits OI margin (2.5% vs. 4.2% prior year); benchmarked against peer MCOs (CI, HUM, MOH) in downside rerating environments.

carelon_rx.rationale: PBM-comparable multiple anchored to Express Scripts pre-Cigna-acquisition (~10-12x OI) and CVS Caremark PBM segment, reflecting ELV captive-mix dependency (~60%+ internal), competitive specialty pharmacy dynamics, and near-term headwinds from lower health plan membership.

carelon_services.rationale: Health-services / tech-enabled multiple benchmarked against Optum Health (pre-composite UNH ~15-20x OI), reflecting Carelon Services' risk-based care model growth, CareBridge acquisition, 33% revenue growth, and long-term margin expansion potential toward management's mid-single-digit target.

back_solve.narrative: At the current EV of ~$88.2B, with Health Benefits valued at the base multiple (8.5x × $4.2B OI = $35.70B), the market is implicitly assigning $52.50B of residual EV to Carelon — implying a blended Carelon EV/OI multiple of 15.44x vs. our base case of 12.16x (11x CarelonRx + 15x Carelon Services blended). The market is implying Carelon at 15.44x EV/OI vs. our base case of 12.16x — a premium of 27.0%.

display_summary: A Sum-of-Parts analysis of Elevance Health (ELV) across three segments — Health Benefits, CarelonRx, and Carelon Services — yields a base-case implied share price of $242.05, representing -17.3% downside to the March 31, 2026 closing price of $292.75. The bull case (10x / 13x / 18x multiples) implies $305.96 per share (+4.5%), while the bear case reaches only $178.15 (-39.1%), underscoring meaningful downside risk from compressed Health Benefits margins and unresolved Medicaid/CMS headwinds. Back-solving from the current ~$88.2B EV, the market is assigning an implied blended Carelon EV/OI of 15.44x — a 27.0% premium to our base-case blended Carelon multiple of 12.16x — suggesting investors are pricing in significant Carelon re-rating optionality that has yet to be earned through margin execution.

sources.description: Elevance Health Q4 & FY2025 Earnings Release (Jan 28, 2026) — segment OI, revenue, margins
SEC EDGAR filing — ELV Q4 2025 Earnings Release (Jan 28, 2026)
Elevance Health Investor Relations — Q4 2025 Earnings Press Release
Share price $292.75 and market cap ~$64.5B as of March 31, 2026 (locked user inputs)
Net debt ~$23.7B implied from EV ~$88.2B minus market cap ~$64.5B (locked user inputs)
